differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune/Rheumatologic condition (e.g., Sarcoidosis or Giant Cell Arteritis)
Drug-induced hepatitis and cardiac conduction disturbance
next_steps: Perform transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for wall motion abnormalities, structural heart disease, and pericardial effusion
Obtain Lyme disease serology (IgM and IgG) and western blot
Obtain specialized infectious disease testing including Q fever serology (Coxiella burnetii) and Bartonella henselae serology
Perform continuous cardiac telemetry monitoring to evaluate for progression of AV block
Consider cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (cMRI) to evaluate for myocardial inflammation or fibrosis
Discontinue herbal supplements and substances potentially contributing to hepatotoxicity
Consult cardiology for assessment of potential conduction system disease

differential_diagnoses: Toxic hepatitis and conduction system injury related to herbal supplements
next_steps: Perform a transthoracic echocardiogram to evaluate for structural heart disease and myocardial wall motion abnormalities
Order comprehensive infectious serologies including Lyme (Western blot), Q fever (Coxiella phase I and II antibodies), and Bartonella species
Perform continuous cardiac telemetry to monitor for progression of AV block and arrhythmias
Obtain cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (cMRI) with late gadolinium enhancement to assess for occult inflammation or fibrosis
Discontinue all herbal and animal-derived supplements pending toxicological assessment
Consult cardiology for assessment of potential pacemaker requirement and management of conduction disease
Collect blood cultures to rule out culture-negative endocarditis
